mapper.sql: id::text AS id, login_id AS "loginId", email, name, phone, user_type AS "userType", failed_login_count AS "failedLoginCount", locked_until AS "lockedUntil", created_at AS "createdAt", updated_at AS "updatedAt"
mapper.select: SELECT id::text AS id FROM "user" ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1
SELECT , password_hash AS "passwordHash" FROM "user" WHERE login_id = #{loginId} LIMIT 1
SELECT , password_hash AS "passwordHash" FROM "user" WHERE email = #{email} LIMIT 1
mapper.insert: INSERT INTO "user" (login_id, email, password_hash, name, phone, user_type) VALUES (#{loginId}, #{email}, #{passwordHash}, #{name}, #{phone}, COALESCE(#{userType}, 'MEMBER')) RETURNING
INSERT INTO user_permission (user_id, permission_id) SELECT ${userId}, p.id FROM permission p WHERE p.use_yn = 'Y' AND p.code = #{userType} ON CONFLICT (user_id, permission_id) DO NOTHING
mapper.update: UPDATE "user" SET failed_login_count = failed_login_count + 1, locked_until = CASE WHEN failed_login_count + 1 >= #{maxFailures} THEN current_kst_timestamp() + (#{lockSeconds} || ' seconds')::interval ELSE locked_until END, updated_at = current_kst_timestamp() WHERE login_id = #{loginId} RETURNING failed_login_count AS "failedLoginCount", locked_until AS "lockedUntil"
UPDATE "user" SET failed_login_count = 0, locked_until = NULL, updated_at = current_kst_timestamp() WHERE id = ${id}
mapper.insert: INSERT INTO refresh_token (user_id, token_hash, user_agent, expires_at, last_used_at) VALUES (${userId}, #{tokenHash}, #{userAgent}, #{expiresAt}::timestamptz, current_kst_timestamp()) RETURNING id::text AS id, user_id::text AS "userId", expires_at AS "expiresAt", last_used_at AS "lastUsedAt", created_at AS "createdAt"
mapper.select: SELECT id::text AS id, user_id::text AS "userId", expires_at AS "expiresAt", last_used_at AS "lastUsedAt", revoked_at AS "revokedAt" FROM refresh_token WHERE token_hash = #{tokenHash} LIMIT 1
mapper.update: UPDATE refresh_token SET revoked_at = current_kst_timestamp() WHERE token_hash = #{tokenHash} AND (revoked_at IS NULL)
